expectations.text: CLAUDE.md was modified
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 6 states 'Lines: 17 → 22'. The output CLAUDE.md in outputs/ confirms new content was added, bringing the file from 17 to 22 lines.

expectations.text: The added content mentions 'host.docker.internal'
expectations.evidence: The new Docker section reads: 'use `host.docker.internal` instead (e.g. `http://host.docker.internal:3000`)'. The exact term appears in outputs/CLAUDE.md.

expectations.text: The added content captures that localhost doesn't work inside containers
expectations.evidence: The added line explicitly states: 'containers cannot reach the host via `localhost`' and '`curl localhost:3000` inside a container will always fail.'

expectations.text: Learning is placed in a relevant section rather than appended randomly
expectations.evidence: The skill created a new '## Docker' section — a purpose-specific heading — rather than appending to an unrelated section or the bottom of the file.

expectations.text: Skill summarizes what was added and where
expectations.evidence: Step 6 of the transcript provides a clear summary: 'Config files modified: CLAUDE.md', 'Added new section: ## Docker', 'Lines: 17 → 22', 'Skills created: None', 'Skills updated: None'.

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md was modified
expectations.evidence: Input CLAUDE.md has 15 lines; output CLAUDE.md has 20 lines (added a ## Docker section with 3 lines of content plus spacing).

expectations.text: The added content mentions 'host.docker.internal'
expectations.evidence: Output CLAUDE.md contains 'host.docker.internal' in both bullet points: 'Use `host.docker.internal` instead (e.g. `http://host.docker.internal:3000`).' and 'use `curl host.docker.internal:3000`'.

expectations.text: The added content captures that localhost doesn't work inside containers
expectations.evidence: Output CLAUDE.md states: '`curl localhost:3000` from inside a container will always fail on macOS' and 'containers cannot reach the host machine via `localhost`'.

expectations.text: Learning is placed in a relevant section rather than appended randomly
expectations.evidence: A dedicated '## Docker' section was created, consistent with the existing heading structure. The transcript notes this was chosen because the file already had a Docker Compose entry in Tech Stack, making a Docker section a natural fit for operational gotchas.

expectations.text: Skill summarizes what was added and where
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly describes what was added ('## Docker section with two bullet points'), where it was placed ('new ## Docker section'), and the reasoning for placement ('natural fit — consistent with the existing heading structure').

expectations.text: A new skill file is created rather than just appending the full workflow to CLAUDE.md
expectations.evidence: The skill file was created at outputs/skills/deploy/SKILL.md with a complete skill definition. CLAUDE.md was also updated, but the workflow content went into the skill file, not CLAUDE.md. The transcript confirms: 'Skills created: /Users/greg/code/agent-skills/learn-workspace/iteration-1/eval-1-deploy-workflow/with_skill/skills/deploy/SKILL.md'.

expectations.text: Created skill includes the key workflow steps
expectations.evidence: The SKILL.md contains 5 numbered steps: '### 1. Build for Production', '### 2. Run Database Migrations', '### 3. Deploy', '### 4. Verify Health', '### 5. Troubleshoot 503'. The 4 original workflow items are all covered (the skill adds a 5th step for 503 troubleshooting which was mentioned in the input). The numbered format matches the requirement.

expectations.text: Created skill has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md lines 1-7 contain valid YAML frontmatter with 'name: deploy' and a multi-line 'description' field using the block scalar '>' syntax. The frontmatter is properly delimited by '---' markers.

expectations.text: build:prod, db:migrate:prod, and /healthz check all appear in the created skill
expectations.evidence: All three appear in SKILL.md: (1) 'npm run build:prod' in step 1 bash block; (2) 'DATABASE_URL=<prod-database-url> npm run db:migrate:prod' in step 2 bash block; (3) 'curl -f https://<host>/healthz' in step 4 bash block.

expectations.text: CloudWatch log group /app/prod troubleshooting tip is included
expectations.evidence: In SKILL.md step 5 (Troubleshoot 503): '- Log group: `/app/prod`' with the context 'If `/healthz` returns 503, check CloudWatch logs first'.

expectations.text: A new skill file is created rather than just appending the full workflow to CLAUDE.md
expectations.evidence: File outputs/skills/deploy/SKILL.md was created. Transcript confirms: 'Files Created: outputs/skills/deploy/SKILL.md — new skill defining the full deployment workflow'. The executor also updated CLAUDE.md but created a separate skill file.

expectations.text: Created skill includes the key workflow steps
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md contains 5 numbered steps (### 1. Build for Production, ### 2. Run Database Migrations, ### 3. Deploy, ### 4. Verify Health, ### 5. Troubleshoot 503 Errors). All 4 user-provided workflow items are covered: build:prod, db migration, /healthz check, and CloudWatch troubleshooting. The 5th step is an expansion of item 4 (troubleshooting) into its own step.

expectations.text: Created skill has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
expectations.evidence:
SKILL.md lines 1-4: '---
name: deploy
description: Deploy the Node.js application to production, including build, database migration, and health verification steps.
---'. Both required fields are present and valid.

expectations.text: build:prod, db:migrate:prod, and /healthz check all appear in the created skill
expectations.evidence: All three are present in SKILL.md: 'npm run build:prod' in Step 1 (line 16), 'npm run db:migrate:prod' in Step 2 (line 26), and '/healthz' in Step 4 (lines 39, 46).

expectations.text: CloudWatch log group /app/prod troubleshooting tip is included
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 5 (lines 44-54) contains: 'If /healthz returns 503 Service Unavailable, check the CloudWatch logs first: Log group: /app/prod' with an aws logs tail command.

expectations.text: Transcript shows both CLAUDE.md and copilot-instructions.md were detected before any files were modified
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1 confirms detection of both files: 'CLAUDE.md — 10 lines' and '.github/copilot-instructions.md — 4 lines'. However, the skill did not ask which to update. It states 'Multiple configs detected. Per task instructions, selected CLAUDE.md as the primary config target for non-interactive execution.' No user prompt or question was presented — the selection was made unilaterally.

expectations.text: The 5-step 'add endpoint' workflow is routed to a new skill, not appended to a config file
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 3 routing table shows 'Add API endpoint workflow | 5 | No | No | Route C → New skill'. The output confirms creation of skills/add-api-endpoint/SKILL.md containing all 5 steps (Create handler, Register route, Add tests, Verify tests, Update docs). CLAUDE.md was not modified to include this workflow.

expectations.text: The conftest.py discovery rule is captured somewhere
expectations.evidence: The output CLAUDE.md contains a new Testing section: 'pytest fixtures must be in `conftest.py` at the project root, not inside individual test directories — otherwise pytest will not discover them'. This matches the original learning verbatim.

expectations.text: The 'run docker compose up db before tests' prerequisite is captured
expectations.evidence: The output CLAUDE.md Commands section contains: 'Run `docker compose up db` before running any tests — tests fail with connection refused if the database is not running'. This directly captures the prerequisite.

expectations.text: Transcript shows a plan or summary of proposed changes (what will be added, where) before any file write operations occur
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4 does present all proposed changes before applying them (listing each change, destination, and line count impact). However, it then immediately states 'Non-interactive mode: applied all changes.' without waiting for or soliciting confirmation. The expectation requires asking for confirmation; none was asked.

expectations.text: Transcript shows both CLAUDE.md and copilot-instructions.md were detected before any files were modified
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows the agent automatically updated both files without asking. Under 'Decisions Made', it states: 'All three learnings are relevant to both Claude Code and Copilot, so both config files were updated.' No question was posed to the user about which files to update.

expectations.text: The 5-step 'add endpoint' workflow is routed to a new skill, not appended to a config file
expectations.evidence: The 5-step workflow was appended directly to both CLAUDE.md (under '## Adding a New API Endpoint') and .github/copilot-instructions.md. The transcript's 'Decisions Made' table explicitly shows 'New API endpoint workflow | Yes (new section) | Yes (new section)'. No new skill file was created anywhere in the outputs.

expectations.text: The conftest.py discovery rule is captured somewhere
expectations.evidence: Captured in both config files. CLAUDE.md Testing section: 'pytest fixtures must be placed in `conftest.py` at the project root, not inside individual test subdirectories — otherwise pytest will not discover them.' Identical content in copilot-instructions.md Testing Setup section.

expectations.text: The 'run docker compose up db before tests' prerequisite is captured
expectations.evidence: Captured in both config files. CLAUDE.md Testing section: 'Always run `docker compose up db` before running tests — tests will fail with "connection refused" if the database is not running.' Also in Commands section and in copilot-instructions.md.

expectations.text: Transcript shows a plan or summary of proposed changes (what will be added, where) before any file write operations occur
expectations.evidence: The transcript presents the final state of modified files only as a summary after decisions were made — not as a preview before editing. There is no evidence of a confirmation step or any user interaction where the agent paused to ask before making changes. The 'Decisions Made' section reads as a retrospective, not a prospective proposal.

notes: Evals 0 and 1 (single config, simple routing): both with_skill and without_skill pass all 5 assertions — Claude naturally persists learnings and creates skills for multi-step workflows even without explicit guidance.
Eval 2 (multi-config, mixed routing, confirmation step): with_skill scores 3/5, without_skill scores 2/5 — the skill provides a meaningful improvement on the hardest scenarios.

The skill's primary value is in eval 2: it correctly routes the 5-step workflow to a new skill file, whereas without_skill appends it to CLAUDE.md. Both configurations fail on detection-before-modification and shows-plan-before-applying assertions.

Assertion 'detects-multiple-configs' (eval 2): both configurations detected both files, but neither paused to ask the user which to update before proceeding — the skill selected CLAUDE.md unilaterally, without_skill updated both automatically.
The skill adds ~34s and ~9000 tokens overhead per run, primarily from reading SKILL.md and following the structured detection/routing/confirmation process.

Token variance in without_skill is high (stddev ~7300) because the without_skill eval 0 run used only 636 tokens — reflecting that without structure, the agent may acknowledge learnings verbally without persisting them to disk.
